example_prompt: Initiate a mobile money or card payment and redirect the customer to complete checkout.

amount.description: Amount to collect. Required when not paying an existing invoice, payment link, or order.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Strongly recommended. Example: "SN", "CI", "GN", "CM", "NG".

paymentReference.description: Your internal order reference. Returned in webhook and verify_transaction so you can match the payment to your order.

merchantReference.description: Optional idempotency or trace reference (UUID format recommended).

successRedirectUrl.description: HTTPS URL to redirect the customer after successful payment.

errorRedirectUrl.description: HTTPS URL to redirect the customer on payment failure.

invoiceId.description: 24-digit invoice ID. Pass only when collecting payment for an existing Bictorys invoice.

paymentLinkId.description: UUID of an existing payment link. Pass only when collecting via a payment link.

orderId.description: 24-digit order ID. Pass only when collecting payment for an existing Bictorys order.

customerId.description: Existing customer UUID. Takes precedence over customerObject when provided.

customerObject.description: Customer details when no customerId is available.

phone.description: International-format phone number as a numeric value. Example: 221700000000.

authorization.description: Set true to reserve (authorize) funds without immediate capture. Requires a separate capture call.

allowUpdateCustomer.description: If true, updates the existing customer profile with data from customerObject. Default: false.

deviceId.description: Device serial number. Required when initiating a payment from a Bictorys physical terminal.

otp.description: One-time password for Orange Money Côte d'Ivoire. The customer must dial #144*82# on their Orange CI phone to generate a 6-8 digit code before submitting the charge.

response_schema.description: Response shape depends on whether payment_type is specified in the query string. HTTP 201 (payment_type given) returns ConfirmationLinkObject for the direct flow. HTTP 202 (no payment_type) returns CheckoutLinkObject for the hosted-checkout flow.

transactionId.description: Unique transaction identifier. Store before redirecting — required for verify_transaction.

redirectUrl.description: URL to redirect the customer (mobile money confirmation page or card 3DS flow).

merchantReference.description: Echo of your merchantReference if provided.

link.description: Mobile money confirmation URL or deep link.

qrCode.description: Base64-encoded QR code image for mobile money confirmation.

message.description: Optional human-readable instruction sent to the customer (e.g. "You will receive an SMS with instructions").

state.description: Card payment state. Present only for CardPaymentObject.

3ds.description: 3D Secure authentication data for card payments. Null for mobile money payments.

3ds2.description: 3D Secure 2 authentication data for card payments. Null for mobile money payments.

link.description: Bictorys-hosted checkout page. Redirect the customer here to select their payment method.

chargeId.description: Unique charge identifier. Store before redirecting.

opToken.description: Operation token embedded in the checkout link.

status.description: HTTP status code as a string. Example: "400".

details.description: Bictorys-specific error code and message. Examples: "E400-6: Amount is missing", "E400-24: Selected Currency is not yet supported for this country", "E400-33: Wrong payment type or disabled".

gotchas: payment_type is a query parameter, not a request body field. Append it to the URL: POST /pay/v1/charges?payment_type=wave_money. Omitting it returns HTTP 202 with a hosted-checkout link instead of a direct 201 response.

The response HTTP status determines the flow: 201 means the direct payment was initiated (use response.transactionId and response.redirectUrl); 202 means the customer must be sent to response.link on the Bictorys-hosted checkout page.
Store transactionId (201 flow) or chargeId (202 flow) before redirecting the customer. Both are required to verify the payment server-side via verify_transaction.
Always verify the transaction server-side via verify_transaction before fulfilling any order. Redirect callbacks and webhooks can be spoofed.
successRedirectUrl and errorRedirectUrl must start with https:// — HTTP URLs are rejected with E400-0: redirectUrl must start with https://.
Sandbox base URL is https://api.test.bictorys.com — the production base URL is https://api.bictorys.com. Sandbox and production use separate API keys obtained from the Bictorys dashboard.
Card payments via the direct API (payment_type=card with a cardObject in the body) require PCI-DSS certification. Mobile money payments have no certification requirement.

create_charge uses the PUBLIC API key (env BICTORYS_PUBLIC_KEY, header X-Api-Key). Using the private/secret key for charges returns 403. Payouts use the private key (BICTORYS_SECRET_KEY, header X-API-Key).

Orange Money Côte d'Ivoire requires an OTP: the customer must dial #144*82# on their phone to generate a 6-8 digit code, then pass it as the 'otp' field in the request body. The OTP expires quickly — if the charge fails with an OTP error, ask the user to redial.

The request body field is 'errorRedirectUrl' (lowercase e), but Bictorys echoes it back as 'ErrorRedirectUrl' (capital E) in some response and webhook contexts. Use lowercase when building the request.
HTTP 403 with an HTML body (not JSON) means AWS WAF is rate-limiting your requests. Implement exponential backoff with at least 5-second spacing between retries.
